context: Checkpoint 2026-04-29 after continuing post-compaction streaming lane and validation-economy work. Host-proxied LLM streaming is live opt-in via LLM_STREAM_RESPONSES=1. Architecture remains: generic SSE framing/reader in tractor::streaming; LLM/provider JSON, usage, tool-call synthesis and AgentResponse projection in Tractor llm_stream_events; provider credentials and route enforcement stay host-owned.
current_tasks: Latest commits after prior handoff: d2094a7c docs(dev): reinforce validation economy; d214a33f feat(tractor): preserve streamed usage counts; 23eb0e05 docs(agent): document streaming opt-in; 1c945b88 docs(agent): note streamed usage preservation; d23c7991 test(agent): assert streamed usage records.

Validation-economy reinforced in AGENTS.md and docs/local-disk-hygiene.md. New package scripts: npm run agent:streaming:check, npm run agent:streaming:harness, npm run agent:streaming:harness:build. Guidance: use filtered tests per slice; rebuild pi_agent.wasm only when pi-agent/WIT changed and harness must execute; clean:light only at checkpoints/low disk.

New user-facing doc docs/agent-streaming.md describes LLM_STREAM_RESPONSES opt-in, response shape, host-owned credentials/route enforcement, partial delta semantics, final assembled response semantics, and economical validation commands. docs/INDEX.md links it and local disk hygiene.

Tractor stream final assembly now preserves provider usage events from SSE. OpenAI-compatible usage fields prompt_tokens/completion_tokens/total_tokens are accumulated from root usage objects. Anthropic input_tokens/output_tokens are accumulated from root or message usage objects. Synthesized final provider JSON now carries usage when present instead of always zero.

Tests added/updated: store_stream_agent_response_chunks_from_reader_preserves_openai_usage; anthropic synthesis test now checks nonzero input/output usage; harness_streaming_opt_in_stores_partials_and_final_response now also asserts UsageRecord tokens_in=13/tokens_out=5 from streamed usage SSE.

Recent validations passed: npm run agent:streaming:check; npm run agent:streaming:harness; npm run project:validate --silent; git diff --check. Afterward ran npm run clean:light and npm run clean:rust:check.
blockers: No GitHub Actions run yet for the long local stack; origin/develop remains behind all streaming/validation-economy commits.
Disk remains tight: after clean:light ~11GB free, Rust targets ~5.1GB.
Full tractor-ts unit suite remains noisy locally due missing WASM fixtures; avoid broad TS suite unless addressing fixtures.
pi-lens/LSP monitor repeatedly crashed on large Rust/markdown edits with memory access out of bounds; cargo checks/tests passed. Mention if diagnosing tooling.
Streaming is live but opt-in only via LLM_STREAM_RESPONSES; default behavior remains buffered/non-streaming.
next_actions: If ready to share, push the stack and watch CI using gh run watch --exit-status. Before push: git status --short; git log --oneline origin/develop..HEAD; npm run agent:streaming:check; npm run agent:streaming:harness; npm run project:validate --silent; git diff --check.

Consider a follow-up harness or unit tests for Anthropic streamed usage/tool_use end-to-end if an Anthropic harness path can be added cheaply without credentials. Unit coverage exists for Anthropic tool_use synthesis and usage shape, but harness path is OpenAI-compatible/ollama.

Consider extracting duplicated provider stream parsing between pi-agent provider_runtime/stream_events.rs and Tractor llm_stream_events.rs into a shared target-neutral LLM SSE parser only if worthwhile and atomic.

Client/UI reassembly remains consumer-side: partial AgentResponse.content chunks are deltas, final AgentResponse is full content. Verify WebSocket clients handle is_final/sequence correctly before claiming polished UX.
Consider adding CI wrappers that call the new agent:streaming scripts, but .github/workflows is protected and should be changed only with explicit confirmation.
open_questions: Should direct AgentResponse partial storage remain the long-term host projection, or should a generic StreamChunk observation type be introduced and projected to AgentResponse?
Should streamed usage accounting parse more provider-specific event shapes (e.g. OpenAI stream_options/include_usage edge cases, Anthropic cache tokens) now or wait for concrete provider fixtures?
Should LLM_STREAM_RESPONSES be exposed through CLI/config rather than only env?
Should Anthropic streaming tool_use have a dedicated end-to-end harness, or are unit tests enough for now?
When should the large local stack be pushed to origin/develop and CI watched?
key_decisions_pending: Long-term StreamChunk vs direct AgentResponse partials.
Shared parser extraction vs current duplicated parser logic.
Provider-specific usage accounting breadth for SSE streams.
Whether to push current large stack now.
Client reassembly/UX criteria for declaring streaming polished.
